subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Janet's Driveway Snow f(t)=7te^(cos t) and Piecewise g(t) — Accumulation, Rate & Piecewise h(t)
stimulus:
There is no snow on Janet's driveway when snow begins to fall at midnight. From midnight to 9 A.M., snow accumulates on the driveway at a rate modeled by $f(t) = 7te^{\cos t}$ cubic feet per hour, where $t$ is measured in hours since midnight. Janet starts removing snow at 6 A.M. ($t = 6$). The rate $g(t)$, in cubic feet per hour, at which Janet removes snow from the driveway at time $t$ hours after midnight is modeled by
$$g(t) = \begin{cases} 0 & 0 \le t < 6 \\ 125 & 6 \le t < 7 \\ 108 & 7 \le t \le 9 \end{cases}$$

parts.prompt: How many cubic feet of snow have accumulated on the driveway by 6 A.M.?
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\int_0^6 f(t)\, dt = 142.274$ or $142.275$ cubic feet.

parts.prompt: Find the rate of change of the volume of snow on the driveway at 8 A.M.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Rate of change is $f(8) - g(8) \approx -59.582$ or $-59.583$ cubic feet per hour.

scoring_points.description: Correct answer with setup: rate of change $= f(8) - g(8) = f(8) - 108 \approx -59.582$ cubic feet per hour

required_elements: Correct answer with setup: rate of change $= f(8) - g(8) = f(8) - 108 \approx -59.582$ cubic feet per hour

parts.prompt: Let $h(t)$ represent the total amount of snow, in cubic feet, that Janet has removed from the driveway at time $t$ hours after midnight. Express $h$ as a piecewise-defined function with domain $0 \le t \le 9$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $h(t) = \begin{cases} 0 & 0 \le t \le 6 \\ 125(t-6) & 6 < t \le 7 \\ 125 + 108(t-7) & 7 < t \le 9 \end{cases}$

parts.prompt: How many cubic feet of snow are on the driveway at 9 A.M.?
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Amount of snow $= \int_0^9 f(t)\, dt - h(9) \approx 26.334$ or $26.335$ cubic feet.
